name: Support / Resistance
description: Level-reaction model that trades clean break, reclaim, or rejection behavior around well-defined support and resistance zones.

context: Daily or weekly highs and lows, prior session levels, and untouched reaction zones mapped.
Level carrying liquidity or repeated reaction history.
Reaction type defined in advance as breakout, reclaim, or rejection.
setup: Price approaching a defined support or resistance with clean swings.
Sweep or rejection printing at the level.
Compression around the zone defining invalidation cleanly.
trigger: Support reclaim or failed move beyond resistance for rejection trades.
Close and hold beyond the level for breakout trades.
Level reaction visibly clear with a defined failure point.
confirmation: Multiple touches, liquidity, or volume making the level meaningful.
Wick rejection or closing behavior confirming the level response.
Next price path open enough for development.
invalidation: Acceptance through the level with hold.
Range expansion through the zone against the thesis.
Two-sided whipsaw on both sides of the zone.
noTrade: Level already overused and reactions decaying.
Stronger nearby level crowding the path.
Messy approach with no clean boundary.

stopLoss: Protective stop beyond the far side of the zone or sweep extreme.
Full zone width used when defining invalidation.
Zone width remaining inside planned tolerance.
takeProfit: Primary target at the next clear level or liquidity pool.
Runner valid only while breakout acceptance or clean rejection continuation persists.
timeExit: Price leaving the zone quickly after entry.
Liquidity-dependent reaction closed before session end.
Trade closed once the level has been fully consumed.
earlyExit: Repeated post-entry tests eroding the reaction edge.
